ObjectStates.Name: CardCustom
ObjectStates.LuaScript:
URL = ""
BASE_URL = "https://tts-magic-booster.fly.dev"
-- BASE_URL = "localhost:8080"

-- Where to place the spawned deck relative to the importer (in world units).
SPAWN_OFFSET = 3.0
SPAWN_HEIGHT = 2.0

-- Status message colors for the in-game chat/console.
INFO_COLOR = {r = 0.7, g = 0.85, b = 1.0}
SUCCESS_COLOR = {r = 0.45, g = 1.0, b = 0.45}
ERROR_COLOR = {r = 1.0, g = 0.35, b = 0.35}

-- Prints a status line to all players' in-game chat/console.
function logStatus(message, color)
    printToAll("[Deck Importer] " .. message, color or INFO_COLOR)
end

function setURL(player, value, id)
    URL = value
end

function getDeck()
    if URL == nil or URL == "" then
        logStatus("Please enter a deck URL first.", ERROR_COLOR)
        return
    end

    logStatus("Fetching deck from server...")
    -- raw=true makes the server return the bare TTS object JSON (no envelope).
    WebRequest.post(BASE_URL.."/url/"..url_encode(URL).."?autofix=true&export=false&raw=true", nil, onResponse)
end

function onResponse(webResp)
    if not webResp.is_done then return end
    if webResp.is_error then
        logStatus("Web request failed: " .. tostring(webResp.error), ERROR_COLOR)
        return
    end

    local txt = webResp.text or ""

    -- Error responses are tiny JSON envelopes ({"error":...,"reason":...}); only those need a decode.
    if txt:sub(1, 64):find('"error"', 1, true) then
        local ok, rjson = pcall(function() return JSON.decode(txt) end)
        local reason = (ok and rjson and (rjson.reason or rjson.error)) or "Import failed."
        logStatus("Error: " .. tostring(reason), ERROR_COLOR)
        return
    end

    logStatus("Decoding deck data...")
    local ok, data = pcall(function() return JSON.decode(txt) end)
    if not ok or data == nil then
        logStatus("Failed to decode the deck data returned by the server.", ERROR_COLOR)
        return
    end

    logStatus("Spawning deck...")
    -- spawnObjectData is lighter than spawnObjectJSON. The position override drops the deck
    -- next to the importer instead of at its baked-in coordinates.
    local origin = self.getPosition()
    spawnObjectData({
        data = data,
        position = {
            origin.x + SPAWN_OFFSET,
            origin.y + SPAWN_HEIGHT,
            origin.z
        },
        callback_function = function(spawned)
            logStatus("Deck imported successfully!", SUCCESS_COLOR)
        end
    })
end

function url_encode(str)
    if (str) then
        str = string.gsub (str, "\n", "\r\n")
        str = string.gsub (str, "([^%w %-%_%.%~])",
            function (c) return string.format ("%%%02X", string.byte(c)) end)
        str = string.gsub (str, " ", "+")
    end
    return str
end
ObjectStates.XmlUI:
<InputField
    position="0 -100 -150"
    rotation="0 0 180"
    placeholder="Deckstats URL"
    onValueChanged="setURL"
    characterValidation="None"
>
</InputField>
<Button
    position="0 100 -150"
    rotation="0 0 180"
    width="100"
    height="50"
    onClick="getDeck"
>Import Deck
</Button>
